name: Sierra Cebollera and Acebal de Garagüeta Loop
name_original: Sierra Cebollera, Puerto de Piqueras y Acebal de Garagüeta – circular desde Matute de la Sierra
description: This difficult 38.0-mile mountain biking route from Matute de la Sierra explores the Sierra Cebollera, Puerto de Piqueras, and Acebal de Gar
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Almarza offer access to the rugged landscapes of the Tierras Altas de Soria, a region characterized by varied terrain and mountain vistas. This area in Soria, Spain, provides a diverse network of routes, from ancient paths winding through stone villages to challenging singletrack. Riders can explore environments shaped by historical transhumance and natural features like the Sierra de San Marcos. The region's topography includes deep valleys and barrancos, providing a dynamic backdrop for mountain biking. Best mountain bike trails around Almarza The most popular mountain bike trail is Sierra Cebollera and Acebal de Garagüeta Loop, a 38.0 miles (61.1 km) trail that takes 5 hours 35 minutes to complete. This difficult route traverses rugged mountain terrain and dense forests. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Sotillo del Rincón loop from Valdeavellano de Tera, a difficult 30.3 miles (48.8 km) path. It offers challenging climbs and descents through the Soria highlands. Local mountain bikers also love the Sotillo del Rincón loop from Caldrejo, a 32.3 miles (52.0 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 8 minutes. Mountain biking around Almarza is defined by rugged terrain, mountain vistas, and ancient paths, with routes passing through stone villages. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Almarza are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from more than 5 reviews. More than 150 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Almarza's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Almarza?
A: Mountain bike trails around Almarza traverse the rugged landscapes of the Tierras Altas de Soria. You'll encounter varied terrain, from ancient paths winding through stone villages to challenging singletrack. The region's topography includes deep valleys and barrancos, offering a dynamic and often wild environment for riding.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Almarza?
A: Yes, Almarza offers a range of mountain bike trails, including options suitable for different ability levels. While many routes are moderate to difficult, there are 4 easy trails available, which can be a good starting point for beginners or families looking for a less strenuous ride. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6953486" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sotillo del Rincón loop from Caldrejo&lt;/a&gt; is rated moderate and offers varied landscapes.

Q: What natural features or viewpoints can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: The region offers stunning natural features and mountain vistas. You can enjoy views of the Sierra de San Marcos, the Duero River, and Peñas Altas. Some routes may lead you past unique geological formations or through areas with significant ichnite (fossil footprint) resources. For specific natural monuments, consider exploring highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3992864" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de la Gallina Spring&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3978327" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente del Oro&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or cultural sites accessible by mountain bike?
A: Yes, mountain biking in Almarza and the surrounding Soria province can lead you past rich historical and cultural sites. The region is known for its ancient paths, stone villages, and castles. While riding, you might encounter sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4000052" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañada Honda Rock Art Site&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3936758" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Stone Bridge over the Tera River&lt;/a&gt;. The area also has a strong transhumance heritage, with museums dedicated to this tradition in places like Oncala.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Almarza?
A: The Soria province, where Almarza is located, experiences varied weather. While specific recommendations depend on personal preference, spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures for mountain biking. Summers can be warm, and winters may bring colder conditions, especially in higher elevations. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: Are there circular mountain bike routes available in Almarza?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes around Almarza are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058330778/sierra-cebollera-and-acebal-de-garagueeta-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Cebollera and Acebal de Garagüeta Loop&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14940411" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sotillo del Rincón loop from Valdeavellano de Tera&lt;/a&gt;, which offers challenging climbs and descents.

Q: What are the typical lengths and durations of mountain bike trails in the area?
A: Mountain bike trails around Almarza vary significantly in length and duration. You can find routes ranging from shorter, moderate rides to extensive, difficult excursions. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33870089" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Andrés de Soria loop from Segoviela&lt;/a&gt; is about 28.4 km and takes around 2 hours 35 minutes, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40327864" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paseo de San Prudencio – Soria Viaduct loop from Aeródromo de Garray&lt;/a&gt; is over 72 km and can take nearly 6 hours.

Q: Is parking available at the trailheads for mountain biking?
A: While specific parking information for every trailhead isn't always detailed, many routes in rural areas like Almarza often have informal parking spots near their starting points, especially in or near villages. For routes starting from specific locations, it's advisable to check the individual route details on komoot for any mentioned parking facilities or nearby village parking.

Q: Are there any regulations or permits required for mountain biking in the Almarza region?
A: Generally, mountain biking on public trails in Spain does not require specific permits, but it's always important to respect local regulations, especially when riding through natural parks or protected areas. Always stay on marked trails, respect private property, and be mindful of other trail users and wildlife. There are no specific permits mentioned for general mountain biking in Almarza.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about riding in Almarza?
A: The mountain bike trails around Almarza are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.1 stars from more than 5 reviews. Riders often praise the rugged terrain, stunning mountain vistas, and the opportunity to explore ancient paths and stone villages. The diverse network of routes caters to various skill levels, making it appealing to many.

Q: Are there any huts or shelters along the mountain bike routes for breaks?
A: Yes, the region around Almarza offers some facilities that can serve as shelters or rest stops. You might encounter places like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3956862" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valonsadero Cabin&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5587268" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Turruca Stone Hut&lt;/a&gt;, or other mountain shelters. These can provide a welcome break during longer rides, especially in varying weather conditions.
tips: Beautiful panoramic view of the Cuenca del Razón when we ride along the long-distance path (GR-86), also known as Camino de las Celadillas.

Located in the heart of the Iberian System and rolling along beautiful forest tracks, we come across this mountain refuge that will be great for us in the event of a storm. The views from here are exceptional.

We find ourselves rolling through the beautiful Natural Reserve of Acebal de Garagüeta. It is the largest existing holly forest in the Iberian Peninsula and in southern Europe. A beautiful place in the highlands of Soria where you can breathe peace and nature together with a large number of holly trees.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: This challenging mountain bike route is approximately 61 km long with over 1100 meters of elevation gain. Komoot estimates an average completion time of around 5 hours and 35 minutes, but this can vary significantly based on your fitness level, technical skills, and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Is this mountain biking trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as 'difficult' and is not suitable for beginners. It requires good fitness, advanced riding skills, and experience with varied terrain, including potential technical sections, climbs, and descents.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain and scenery can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The route offers incredibly diverse terrain and stunning scenery. You'll traverse the Acebal de Garagüeta Nature Reserve, the largest holly forest in Southern Europe, and ride through the Sierra de Cebollera Natural Park. Expect a mosaic of dense Scots pine, beech, and oak forests, alternating with pastures and mountain thickets. The landscape also features glacial relief formations and offers magnificent panoramic views, especially from Puerto de Piqueras.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Key highlights include the Acebal de Garagüeta Nature Reserve, known for its enchanting holly forest, and the panoramic View of the Razón Basin (GR-86). You'll also pass a Mountain Shelter on the Trail to Alto de Terrazas and enjoy extensive views from Puerto de Piqueras.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: Autumn is particularly recommended for this route, as the holly trees in the Acebal de Garagüeta Nature Reserve transform into a vibrant display of red and gold, creating a 'fairy tale' atmosphere. Generally, spring and early summer also offer pleasant conditions before the peak heat of summer.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this circular route from Matute de la Sierra?
faqs.answer: As this is a circular route starting and ending in Matute de la Sierra, you should be able to find parking within or on the outskirts of the village. Specific parking areas are not detailed, but local signage or asking residents upon arrival should guide you to suitable spots.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this mountain biking trail?
faqs.answer: The route passes through the Sierra de Cebollera Natural Park and the Acebal de Garagüeta Nature Reserve. While dogs are generally allowed in natural parks in Spain, they must typically be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other visitors. Always check specific park regulations before your visit.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: There are no general entrance fees for the Sierra de Cebollera Natural Park or the Acebal de Garagüeta Nature Reserve. However, specific activities or access to certain sensitive areas might require permits. For mountain biking, it's generally free, but always respect local regulations and signage, especially within protected areas.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I encounter along the trail?
faqs.answer: The Sierra de Cebollera and Acebal de Garagüeta are rich in biodiversity. You might spot amphibians, deer, foxes, and wild boars. Birdwatchers could be lucky enough to see birds of prey like griffon vultures and golden eagles soaring overhead.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with any other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route briefly intersects with several other paths. You'll find small sections that overlap with [CIMA SO08] Piqueras, [CIMA LR13] Piqueras, Ruta romana del iregua tramo 5, and Camino de Castellanos.

faqs.question: Are there any accommodations or places to eat near Matute de la Sierra?
faqs.answer: Matute de la Sierra is a small village, so options might be limited directly within it. However, the surrounding region, particularly larger towns in La Rioja and Soria, offers various accommodations, restaurants, and cafes. It's advisable to check for services in nearby towns like Vinuesa or El Royo, or even Logroño, depending on your travel plans.

waypoints.name: Mountain Shelter on the Trail to Alto de Terrazas
